hooks.text: You paid your supplier in Yuan. They never received Yuan. Somewhere between you and them, your money switched to US dollars.
There are exactly three spots where your Yuan payment can quietly flip into US dollars before it reaches your supplier. Most people never find them.
You sent Yuan once. But it can get converted three separate times before your supplier sees it. Three hidden currency cuts on one payment.
A business owner pays a factory in Yuan. The factory opens its account and sees US dollars. Now they have to convert it back, and pay for it.
Every time you pay a Chinese supplier in Yuan, you might be funding two currency swaps you can't see. That cost comes off your margin, quietly.
